senses.definition: plural of bordel, a French-derived term used in English to refer to a brothel, a place where sex work is offered.
examples: The old city alley was once lined with bordels.
Rumors about the bordels permeability circulated among traders.

senses.definition: the outer edge or boundary of an object, place, or area.
examples: Paint along the border of the wall.
The country shares a border with another nation.

senses.definition: to be along or form the edge of something; to touch the edge.
examples: The garden borders the river.
A silver frame borders the photograph.

senses.definition: a decorative strip around or along the edge of something.
examples: a gold border on the frame
the border of the notebook

senses.definition: a detailed inventory or list of goods or documents, often used in business or insurance contexts.
examples: The ship's manifests were attached to the bordereau.
Insurance required a bordereau of all claims.

senses.definition: plural of bordereau; multiple inventories or lists of goods or claims.
examples: The insurer reviewed several bordereaux before settling claims.

senses.definition: having a border or edge; marked with a border.
examples: A bordered notebook sits on the desk.
The bordered map clearly shows the coastline.

senses.definition: a person who lives near or guards a border; someone who forms or marks a border.
examples: The borderer watched the frontier with caution.

senses.definition: plural of borderer; multiple people who guard or live near borders.
examples: The borderers were stationed at multiple checkpoints.

senses.definition: an obscure or historical term; not widely used in modern English. may refer to borders or borderlands in some lexicons.
examples: Historical texts mention borderies in colonial regimes.

senses.definition: to lie along or be next to the edge of something.
examples: The fields bordering the river are flooded.
A line of trees bordering the road provides shade.

senses.definition: the act of forming a border; the edge that separates areas.
examples: There is heavy bordering between the two zones.

senses.definition: plural of bordering; instances of forming borders or edges.
examples: The borderings along the coast are difficult to traverse.

senses.definition: a term lacking common usage; may refer to a doctrine or practice related to borders in certain contexts.
examples: Borderism is rarely used in modern texts.

senses.definition: a region that lies on or near a border between two areas or countries.
examples: The borderland between two nations often has mixed culture.

senses.definition: a person living in a borderland or who crosses borders; uncommon in everyday use.
examples: Borderlanders navigate rugged terrain daily.

senses.definition: areas around a border; also a region of ambiguous jurisdiction or culture.
examples: Many novels are set in the borderlands between countries.

senses.definition: without borders; not restricted by boundaries or limits.
examples: A borderless world would require new ways to manage trade.

senses.definition: an uncommon or technical term; may refer to a light used at borders or a decorative edge light.
examples: The borderlight illuminated the boundary line.

senses.definition: a line showing the border between two areas; a boundary that is not clear.
examples: The borderlines between zones are poorly marked.

senses.definition: very close to a particular condition or limit; nearly failing or succeeding.
examples: Her grades were borderline between pass and fail.
Borderline acceptable weather for outdoors.

senses.definition: plural of borderline; multiple lines or cases indicating boundaries or thresholds.
examples: The borderlines between regions were marked clearly.

senses.definition: a mark or sign indicating a border.
examples: The bordermark showed the territorial limit.

senses.definition: plural of border; the lines separating countries, states, or regions.
examples: They crossed several borders during their trip.

senses.definition: an uncommon term referring to the side of a border or boundary.
examples: The camp lay on the borderside.

senses.definition: possibly a proper noun or misspelling; in several languages, a surname or place name; not a common English term.
examples: Bordes is a surname in some regions.

senses.definition: a surname or a locomotive term; not common in everyday English.
examples: Mr. Bordman chaired the meeting.

senses.definition: an archaic or rare term; related to drilling or bordage in some crafts; not widely used in modern English.
examples: A bordrag technique was described in old manuals.

senses.definition: a historical or technical term; may refer to edging or fastening techniques in crafts or weaponry.
examples: The bordrage preserved the edge of the blade.
